dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 8 for a seizure call at an apartment in [CITY].
Hi, I'm Mike. I'm a paramedic. What made you call 911 today?
My boyfriend had a seizure, just now. He's lying in the bathroom.
Mr. Smith, can you tell me your name?
Yeah, Mike Smith.
What year is it right now?
I think... 2020.
Where are we, sir?
Apartment 203. In [CITY].
And what happened today?
I had a seizure. I don't remember the details.
Both eyes are open and responsive.
What was the last thing you remember before the seizure?
I remember walking into the bathroom... then nothing.
He responded when I touched his arm.
We're going to check your airway now. Okay?
Airway is clear.

dialogue.micro_intent: check breathing - Rate and depth of respirations
dialogue.utterance: 20 respirations per minute, regular depth.

dialogue.micro_intent: check breathing - Oxygen saturation measured with pulse oximetry
dialogue.utterance: SpO2 is 97%, holding steady.

dialogue.micro_intent: check breathing - Presence of bilateral breath sounds
dialogue.utterance: Breath sounds are clear and equal bilaterally.

dialogue.micro_intent: check circulation - Pulse rate and quality
dialogue.utterance: Palpable pulse, pulse is 50, slow but present.
Blood pressure is 155 over 81.
Skin is pink and warm.
Are you experiencing any pain or other symptoms right now?
I have a headache and feel a bit weak.
Do you have a history of allergies or adverse reactions to medication?
No allergies that I know of.
Are you taking any medications right now?
I'm taking Effexor and Vistaril for anxiety.
Do you have a history of seizures or head trauma?
He has epilepsy and has had seizures in the past.
What did you or the patient eat or drink recently?
He had a few beers and some weed last night.

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: Can you describe exactly where and when the seizure occurred?

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: He went into the bathroom and suddenly collapsed.
When did the headache start?
Right after the seizure ended.
What makes the headache feel worse?
It just feels like a continuous pressure.
Describe the sensation of the head pain for me.
It's a low knock on my head, not too bad.
Does the pain go anywhere else in the body?
It's only in my head.
How bad is the pain on a scale of 0 to 10?
Maybe a 3.
How long has the headache been going on?
Since the seizure, maybe a few minutes ago.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Pupillary reflex / Size of pupils
dialogue.utterance: Pupils are equal and reactive, about 4 mm.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - neck veins
dialogue.utterance: No signs of distension or swelling in the neck area.
Heartbeat is slow but otherwise normal.
You're alert and oriented, person, place, time, and event.
Pulsing at 47, still bradycardic.
Breathing is 20 per minute and normal.
Blood pressure is now 141 over 78.
Glucose meter is reading zero-probably not working.
Oxygen is at 98%, still stable.
EKG was initially not applicable, as per the first vital check.
We're attaching a long-term cardiac monitor now. Okay?
4-lead ECG applied successfully on the first try.
Ready for the 12-lead ECG. Don't worry, it's quick.
12-lead ECG attached and interpreted as a sinus arrhythmia.
We're following the medical-seizure protocol (Protocol 3-12).
Pulse remains steady at 47.
Respirations are still 20 and calm.
Blood pressure is now stable at 141 over 78.
Another zero reading on the glucose meter. Probably inaccurate.
SpO2 slightly increased to 98%.
Rhythm is still sinus arrhythmia.
Has your headache changed since we first assessed it?
Still about the same since you asked before.
Any new triggers making the pain worse?
Just the pressure again.
How does the pain feel now?
It's still a dull knock on my head.
Has the pressure moved to any other area?
Still just in my head.
Is the pain still a 3?
Yeah, it's about a 3.
Has the pain been constant or worsening?
It's been pretty consistent.

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - signs and symptoms
dialogue.utterance: Any new symptoms since the seizure?

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - signs and symptoms
dialogue.utterance: No new symptoms reported by the patient or bystander.

dialogue.micro_intent: reorder history of present illness - pertinent past medical history
dialogue.utterance: Do you have a history of seizures, specifically grand mal seizures?

dialogue.micro_intent: reorder history of present illness - pertinent past medical history
dialogue.utterance: Yes, the seizure was grand mal in nature.

dialogue.micro_intent: reorder history of present illness - pertinent past medical history
dialogue.utterance: Was there any aura before the seizure began?

dialogue.micro_intent: reorder history of present illness - pertinent past medical history
dialogue.utterance: No, there was no aura reported.

dialogue.micro_intent: reorder history of present illness - signs and symptoms
dialogue.utterance: Have you experienced any new sensation of lethargy or slowness since the seizure?

dialogue.micro_intent: reorder history of present illness - signs and symptoms
dialogue.utterance: I've felt a bit more tired than usual since it happened.
Skin is warm and sticky. Would you say it feels clammy?
Yeah, my skin feels a bit moist.
Earlier, the glucose was at 117-since the first reading was zero, we'll try another glucose check now.
Second reading gives a result of 117 mg/dL, so it appears the initial zero was an error.
Would you like to go to the hospital?
No, I don't want to go. I'm fine.
Alright, you've decided to refuse transport, Mr. Smith.
We'll assist you back to your bed and complete the refusal form.
Call 911 if any symptoms change or worsen.
We'll carefully move you back to your bed now.
Don't hesitate to reach out if things change later today.
You're transferred and everything's safe now.
You've been given the best care possible. Stay well, Mr. Smith.
We're going to check your GCS now.
Eye opening is to voice, verbal response is oriented to person, place, time, and event, and motor response is to command. AOX4 confirmed.
GCS is 15 (E4 V5 M6).
Does this lethargy feel like a new symptom for you?
Yes, this post-seizure fatigue is new.
Skin is normal and retracts to pinch.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Gross visual examination
dialogue.utterance: Normocephalic and no visible head trauma detected.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Assessment of mental status / Short & Long term memory assessment
dialogue.utterance: What was the last thing you read or told your girlfriend before the seizure?

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Assessment of mental status / Short & Long term memory assessment
dialogue.utterance: I told her I was going to go to the bathroom. That's it.

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam; Partner
dialogue.utterance: No hemorrhage, drainage, or obstruction noted in ENT exam.

dialogue.micro_intent: thorax/lungs/cardio exam - Peripheral vs. Central Pulse Comparison
dialogue.utterance: Radial pulse is consistent with central pulse.
No scars noted on torso or neck.
Abdomen is soft, non-tender, non-distended.
Bowel sounds are within normal limits.
I have not seen him urinate yet.

dialogue.micro_intent: genitourinary exam - Frequency / Urgency of urination
dialogue.utterance: He doesn't seem to have a need for the bathroom.

dialogue.micro_intent: extremities and back exam - Gross motor and sensory function
dialogue.utterance: All extremities move freely and feel normal to touch.

dialogue.micro_intent: extremities and back exam - Peripheral pulses
dialogue.utterance: Dorsalis pedis and posterior tibialis pulses present bilaterally.
No cuts, scrapes, or bruises visible on extremities.
